evidence: V presents the Zo environment, explaining it as a cloud-based server controlled via prompting with a full file system. He contrasts it with the limited file handling of standard ChatGPT.
V demonstrates how Zo automatically processes Fireflies transcripts into intelligence blocks (Recaps, Deliverables, Blurbs) and connects them to task managers.
V runs a live demo of a complex task: researching a contact via Aviato API and drafting a warm outreach email in "Vibe Operator" mode.
Discussion on how Zo replaces multiple subscriptions (ChatGPT, Claude, Apollo, N8N) by running custom instances and using direct APIs.
The Guest identifies a target audience for Zo: founders without CTOs and high-level operators building MVPs.
The Guest proposes a multi-hour AI hackathon focusing on building personal or organizational solutions (e.g., CRM). V suggests a "Hack for Good" framing and involving partners.
V proposes creating a product within Zo that David could promote: A 'networking agent in a box' or 'networking capabilities in a box' that users can flash via GitHub onto their Zo system.
David reflects on what can and cannot be automated. He emphasizes that while the search and tracking can be automated, the 'last mile' (writing the actual message) requires a human in the loop.
V suggests a multi-part series where people follow along on Zo to build their own 'Spiegel.' The goal is a repeatable mechanism that personalizes the tool for the user (the 'IKEA effect').
David identifies three core networking needs: 2. Internal Networking: Cross-functional visibility within large companies. 3. Personal Networking: Staying in touch with past co-workers/friends.
The meeting centered on the launch of the ChatGPT App Store and the implementation of MCP (Model Context Protocol) as the foundational architecture for these apps.
The representative demonstrated the Alpic platform, which functions as a "Vercel for MCP," offering one-click deployment from GitHub with analytics and logs.
V inquired about connecting his existing Zo instance to this framework. He envisions ChatGPT as the frontend, Zo as the backend, and Skybridge as the middle layer/deployer.
Initial banter regarding supplements, health habits, and V's 10k run in Puerto Rico (Feb). Discussion on running clubs vs. indoor running.
